purpose: A modal overlay that interrupts the user flow to present a focused task, confirmation, or information. Blocks interaction with the underlying page until dismissed.
whenToUse: For confirmations of destructive or critical actions
For focused short tasks requiring user input
whenNotToUse: For non-blocking notifications — use Toast or MessageStrip
For long forms — use a new page
doRules: Use for confirmations, focused tasks, or detail views requiring user action
Include primary action button (right) and Cancel (left)
Use State variant for semantic messages (Information, Warning, Error)
Provide title that summarizes the dialog purpose
dontRules: Do not put long forms in Dialog — use a new page
Do not omit close affordance (Cancel button or X)
Do not block escape key from closing

layoutGuidance.placement: Centered over the application; backdrop dims underlying content.
layoutGuidance.sizing: Auto width 320–80% viewport; height hugs content up to 80% viewport.
layoutGuidance.alignment: Title top, content middle, action bar bottom.

contentGuidance.labelLength: Title 1–8 words; body content as needed.
contentRules: Body explains what user must decide

accessibilityGuidance.requirement: Escape closes; Tab cycles within dialog (focus trap)
Focus moves into dialog on open; returns to trigger on close
Dialog has accessible title via aria-labelledby
Destructive dialogs use State=Warning or Error with text — not color alone
Dialog open/close announced; content announced when opened

exceptions: Message type for short confirmations (no custom content)
